---
title = "Installation"
---

Install *Calepin* from source using Cargo:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin
cd calepin
make install
```

This builds the release binary, installs it to `~/.cargo/bin/`, and sets up shell completions.

Check your installation:

```bash
calepin -v
```


# External tools

*Calepin* can call external programs for code execution, PDF compilation, and diagram rendering. All are optional --- only install the ones you need.

Code execution:

- [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) -- R code chunks (`{r}`)
- [python3](https://www.python.org/) -- Python code chunks (`{python}`), requires building with `cargo build --features python`

PDF compilation:

- [tectonic](https://tectonic-typesetting.github.io/) -- compile LaTeX to PDF (`calepin render -t pdf`)
- [typst](https://typst.app/) -- compile Typst to PDF (`calepin render -t pdf --writer typst`)

Diagrams:

- [tectonic](https://tectonic-typesetting.github.io/) -- TikZ diagrams (`{tikz}`). PDF-to-SVG and SVG-to-PDF conversions are handled internally.
- [mmdc](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli) -- Mermaid diagrams (`{mermaid}`)
- [dot](https://graphviz.org/) -- Graphviz diagrams (`{dot}`)
- [d2](https://d2lang.com/) -- D2 diagrams (`{d2}`)

Website features:

- [pagefind](https://pagefind.app/) -- static site search (optional)

# Shell completions

*Calepin* can generate shell completions for tab-completion of commands and options:

```bash
calepin new completions bash > ~/.bash_completion.d/calepin
calepin new completions zsh > ~/.zfunc/_calepin
calepin new completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/calepin.fish
```

Restart your shell or source the file to activate completions.
